feedback.text: I am writing to express my strong support for the Digital Fairness Acts proposal to regulate addictive design practices in video games, and to urge you in particular to address gamblinglike features such as loot boxes. Loot boxes are unregulated gambling Loot boxes promise rare digital items in exchange for real money, yet their randomized mechanics mirror slot machines and other games of chance. Unlike regulated gambling, they carry no requirements for odds disclosure, age checks, spending limits, or coolingoff periodsand they are aggressively marketed to minors. Personal experience: 1,000 lost As a teenager, I frequently played multiplayer games with loot boxes. Initially, small purchases felt harmless, but the variablereward design quickly hooked me. Over several years, I spent more than 1,000 of my pocket money chasing rare items. I did not gain lasting valueonly frustration, wasted funds, and distraction from healthier activities. Had loot boxes been treated like gambling, with transparent odds and spending safeguards, I never would have lost so much. Vulnerability of children and adolescents Adolescents brains are especially susceptible to addictive stimuli and poor impulse control. By embedding lootbox mechanics into games rated suitable for young audiences, developers exploit their vulnerability and normalize gambling behaviors early in life. Requested measures To protect consumersespecially minorsI respectfully ask the EU to: - Classify loot boxes as gambling, subject to existing regulations and oversight. - Mandate prominent odds disclosure for every lootbox tier before purchase. - Require robust age verification and parental consent for randomized ingame purchases. - Set spending caps and coolingoff periods, limiting how much and how quickly players can buy loot boxes. - Ban predatory tactics such as timelimited offers or fear of missing out mechanics targeting children. These measures would introduce transparency, curb exploitative practices, and foster healthier gaming environments. Game developers could still create engaging experiences without relying on manipulative, gamblinglike revenue models. Thank you for considering this feedback. I hope the Digital Fairness Act will set a global standard for protecting playersespecially the young and vulnerablefrom the harms of unregulated, addictive game mechanics.
